stemHtml: Shortly after take-off for a cross-country flight from Walden, with the call sign Fastair 789,one of your passengers informs you about feeling unwell. You are already in the control area of Alexander Control. A message must be sent to Alexander Control. Which option is most accurately in compliance with the standards for messages on this type of situation? "PAN PAN, PAN PAN, PAN PAN.."

options.a: ALEXANDER CONTROL, FASTAIR 789, 20 MILES WEST OF WALDEN, ESTIMATED TIME EN ROUTE 20 MINUTES, ESTIMATED ARRIVAL 35, FL150, TRANSPONDER CHARLIE, ONE SICK PASSENGER ON BOARD, RETURNING TO WALDEN.
options.b: ALEXANDER CONTROL, FASTAIR 789, A PASSENGER IS FEELING UNWELL, RETURNING TO WALDEN, 20 MILES WEST OF WALDEN, FL150, HEADING 280.
options.c: MEDICAL, FASTAIR 789, A PASSENGER IS FEELING UNWELL, RETURNING TO WALDEN, 20 MILES WEST OF WALDEN, FL150, HEADING 280.
options.d: MEDICAL, FASTAIR 789, 20 MILES WEST OF WALDEN, ONE SICK PASSENGER ON BOARD, RETURNING TO WALDEN, ESTIMATED TIME EN ROUTE 20 MINUTES, ESTIMATED ARRIVAL 35, FL150, TRANSPONDER CHARLIE.
